title: Floating Shelf with Hidden Closet Rod
summary: A plain-looking floating shelf that hides five dowel pegs underneath — a disguised place to hang clothes in a room with no real closet.
description:
From the front this is just a floating shelf. From underneath, five short lengths of closet-rod dowel are mounted pointing down, turning it into a hidden set of hanging pegs — a real fix for a guest room or dorm space with no actual closet, without looking like a garment rack bolted to the wall.

The box itself is a simple 1x4 frame topped with a 1x8, so the real work here is getting the dowel spacing even and the whole thing solidly anchored: hangers loaded with clothes put real leverage on the wall mount, more than the shelf's own light weight would suggest, so don't skip driving into studs.

cutList.note: Cut to fit; space evenly along the underside.

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Attach the 1x4 pieces into a rectangle. Use 2" screws at the joints (pocket holes placed on the outside also work if the ends will be hidden), or 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the 1x8 top to the 1x4 frame with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the hidden pegs
steps.body: Cut the closet rod dowel into five pegs and space them evenly along the bottom of the shelf. Attach with 2" screws or 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Finish and hang
steps.body: Finish as desired, then hang to wall studs with screws — adjust screw length to your wall thickness and construction; 3" self-tapping screws suit standard stud-framed walls with 1/2" drywall. Check level before final tightening, since a loaded hanging peg amplifies any lean.

images.alt: Floating wood shelf with hidden hanging pegs underneath
